tweets.name: Paul Graham
tweets.text: Every post, I block and report a couple accounts using AI-generated replies. It feels much the same as picking up litter on a city street; I don't know if it has any effect, but it feels like I ought to at least try to keep the site clean.

tweets.name: Paul Graham
tweets.text: Durer was the original Dr Seuss. https://t.co/UXCDCwNpj1

tweets.name: Paul Graham
tweets.text: Writing a talk to give in Stockholm. The prospect of speaking to a large group of people is no longer frightening, but writing a talk is as much work as ever. I'm no longer afraid of audiences, but I'm still just as afraid of making mistakes.

feels like a good time to seriously rethink how operating systems and user interfaces are designed

(also the internet; there should be a protocol that is equally usable by people and agents)

Software is not a moat

Over the last 15+ years, nearly every innovation @EvanSpiegel and his team shipped got copied. Stories. AR glasses. Swipe-based navigation. The camera-first interface.

And yet @Snapchat is the only independent consumer social app that has lasted. Nearly 1

the thesis: AI assisted devs ship faster but understand nothing. the next generation will be illiterate at the layer that matters.

Guys I’m not dumb - I used a temporary chat so it didn’t use any of my memories / had no context on previous chats!

I want to not believe in palm reading…

But ChatGPT guessed I was an investor or founder (and nailed a bunch of other stuff).

tweets.name: Tim Ferriss
tweets.text:
“To learn which questions are unanswerable, and not to answer them: this skill is most needful in times of stress and darkness.”​
— Ursula K. Le Guin

tweets.name: Tim Ferriss
tweets.text:
“The test of all beliefs is their practical effect in life. If it be true that optimism compels the world forward, and pessimism retards it, then it is dangerous to propagate a pessimistic philosophy.​​”​
— Helen Keller, Optimism

tweets.name: Hiten Shah
tweets.text: “The harder I work, the luckier I get.” https://t.co/fAPT70zmMm

tweets.name: Hiten Shah
tweets.text:
The fastest learners have the fewest excuses between thought and action.

They do the thing. Then they study what happened.

That loop beats almost every form of preparation.

At some point, more input becomes avoidance that feels productive.

tweets.name: Hiten Shah
tweets.text: Agents help you do this, but they don’t do it for you.
